question: Walk me through Stellar's protocol upgrade history from Protocol 19 through the latest version, with the headline feature of each.

golden.answer:
A grounded answer obtains the requested current value or state from the current official Stellar documentation, states the measurement basis, and makes the observation date visible. As of 2026-07-10, the verified sources supported this observed answer:

Stellar protocol history from P19 through live **Protocol 27**: P19 (2022-06-08) generalized transaction preconditions and signed-payload signers; P20/Soroban (2024-02-20) introduced Wasm smart contracts; P21 (2024-06-18) added secp256r1/passkeys and TTL extension; P22 (2024-12-05) added BLS12-381 and constructors; P23/Whisk (2025-09-03) introduced CAP-0063 parallel-scheduling support, CAP-0062 live/archival separation, and unified events—but initialized the dependent-cluster setting at one, with multi-cluster activation handled later through network settings; P24 (2025-10-22) corrected Whisk archival stability; P25/X-Ray (2026-01-22) added BN254 and Poseidon/Poseidon2; P26/Yardstick (2026-05-06) added configuration, arithmetic, TTL-v2, BN254, SAC, and strkey host changes; P27/Zipper (2026-07-08) activated CAP-0071 authentication delegation/address-bound credentials at ledger 63386819. The feature mappings are durable, but re-check the live network for the latest tail and current configuration.

The dated example is illustrative: a later grounded answer should report what the same source basis supports at its own observation time while preserving the stable distinctions above.
keyFacts: Presents specific source-supported content as a current or dated observation rather than as an unsupported memory claim.
Makes the as-of date visible for every changeable roster, status, version, or measurement.
Describes P23 as capability introduction with initial one-cluster configuration, not immediate multi-cluster activation.
avoid: Do NOT mis-pair features and protocol versions.
Do NOT call P27 pending or P26 latest after 2026-07-08, or imply P23 immediately enabled multiple dependent clusters.
Do NOT present version-, provider-, interface-, or availability-sensitive observations as timeless; date the changeable part without weakening stable protocol or historical facts.
golden.notes:
GT-34 RECONCILIATION 2026-07-10: P27/Zipper is live from ledger 63386819 on 2026-07-08. P23 introduced CAP-0063 support with the dependent-cluster setting initially one; later network settings enabled multiple clusters. Grade feature/version mapping and dated live-tail verification. The P20 row says CAP-0046 series and does not imply a contiguous numbered range.
GT-25/GT-34 independently reconciled: P27 live tail and P23 initial-one-cluster nuance are both effective; duplicate override composition was removed.

corroboration.claim: P23 introduced CAP-0063 support with the setting initially one; the latest P27 tail remains unchanged.

Preserved main's 2026-07-10 observation date and prior evidence; this rewording does not claim a 2026-07-11 re-observation.
rootCause: Eval-side compressed P23 headline plus duplicate JSON keys that silently replaced provenance without replacing stale grader notes.
